response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad software engineering background with genuine strengths in distributed systems and software craftsmanship, but he fails the most critical minimum qualification by a wide margin: the role requires 8 years of C++ experience, while his resume shows only approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and C++ does not appear in his skills section at all. Beyond that disqualifying gap, his cloud experience is limited to AWS CDK usage at an application/product layer rather than platform-level infrastructure ownership, and there is no evidence of sustained technical leadership over a cloud compute or infrastructure team.
strengths: Hands-on distributed systems experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using high-performance, low-latency techniques
Consistent software craftsmanship culture across roles: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing, and community knowledge-sharing through workshops and dojos
Polyglot engineering background spanning embedded systems, web, mobile, and infrastructure tooling, demonstrating adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months vs. the 8-year minimum requirement) and is entirely absent from the listed skills section — a hard disqualifier
No evidence of technical leadership over a cloud compute platform or infrastructure engineering team at any scale
Cloud experience is confined to AWS CDK at the application/product layer at Optilyz; no production-level cloud platform architecture ownership or platform engineering is demonstrated
